title: formal "to be"
explanation: Used in formal situations to state identity or facts

title: polite "to be"
explanation: Used in everyday conversation to state identity

title: topic marker
explanation: Marks the topic of the sentence and contrasts information
translation_1: As for me, I am a student

title: subject marker
explanation: Marks the subject of the sentence

title: object marker
explanation: Marks the object of the action

title: time/place marker
explanation: Indicates time or destination

title: location of action
explanation: Indicates where an action takes place

title: and (noun connector)
explanation: Connects nouns meaning "and"

title: also/too
explanation: Indicates "also" or "too"

title: only
explanation: Indicates limitation meaning "only"

title: and (sequence)
explanation: Connects verbs to show actions happening in sequence

title: but/however
explanation: Used to express contrast between two clauses
translation_1: It is raining but I go
translation_2: It is hard but I do it

title: because
explanation: Used to express reason or cause
translation_1: I eat because I am hungry
translation_2: I can’t go because I am busy

title: want to
explanation: Used to express desire to do something
translation_1: I want to go to Korea

title: can/ability
explanation: Used to express ability or possibility

title: please/do
explanation: Used to make polite requests or commands

title: shall we
explanation: Used to suggest doing something together

title: exclamation
explanation: Used to express surprise or realization

title: negation
explanation: Used to make negative sentences

title: while
explanation: Used to express two actions happening at the same time
translation_1: I study while listening to music
translation_2: I talk on the phone while walking

title: intend to
explanation: Used to express intention or plan to do something
translation_1: I intend to go to Korea

title: try doing
explanation: Used to express trying something

title: do for someone
explanation: Used when doing something for someone else

title: progressive
explanation: Used to express an ongoing action

title: must
explanation: Used to express obligation

title: may/allowed
explanation: Used to express permission

title: because
explanation: Used to express reason in formal situations
translation_1: I don’t go because it rains
translation_2: I can’t meet because I am busy

title: before doing
explanation: Used to express doing something before another action
translation_2: I wash my hands before eating

title: after doing
explanation: Used to express doing something after another action
translation_2: I go home after watching a movie

title: have experience
explanation: Used to express past experience

title: completion/regret
explanation: Used to express completion of an action often with regret

title: future
explanation: Used to express future intention or prediction

title: guess/intention
explanation: Used to express guess or strong intention

title: suggestion/question
explanation: Used to suggest or ask opinion

title: nominalization
explanation: Used to turn verbs into nouns

title: in the middle of
explanation: Used to express an action in progress
translation_1: I am in the middle of studying

title: while doing then
explanation: Used when one action is interrupted by another

title: background
explanation: Used to give background or context
translation_1: It is raining, so I don’t go
translation_2: I don’t have time, what should I do?

title: realization
explanation: Used to express something realized from experience

title: when
explanation: Used to express time "when"

title: if
explanation: Used to express a hypothetical condition
translation_1: If I have time, I will go
translation_2: If I have a lot of money, I will travel

title: as soon as
explanation: Used to express immediately after something
translation_1: I slept as soon as I got home
translation_2: He came in as soon as the door opened

title: the more... the more
explanation: Used to show increasing degree
translation_1: The more I study, the more interesting it is
translation_2: The more I think, the harder it gets

title: so that
explanation: Used to express purpose or result
translation_1: Hurry so that you are not late
translation_2: Write big so that it is visible

title: to end up doing
explanation: Used to express a change of state
translation_1: I ended up going to Korea
translation_2: I came to do that work

title: might
explanation: Used to express uncertainty

title: seems like
explanation: Used to make a guess based on observation
translation_2: It seems like that person is busy

title: I think/seems
explanation: Used to express opinion or assumption

title: looks like
explanation: Used to describe appearance

title: tend to
explanation: Used to express general tendency
translation_1: I tend to eat a lot

title: pretend
explanation: Used to pretend doing something

title: while in state
explanation: Used to keep a state while doing another action
translation_1: I slept with the door open
translation_2: He came in with shoes on

title: almost did
explanation: Used to express that something almost happened

title: no way
explanation: Used to strongly deny possibility
translation_2: There is no way he will come

title: no need
explanation: Used to express that something is unnecessary

title: thanks to
explanation: Used to express positive reason
translation_1: Thanks to your help, I succeeded
translation_2: Thanks to the rain, it’s cool

title: because of
explanation: Used to express negative reason
translation_1: I was late because of the rain
translation_2: Because of a mistake, a problem occurred

title: compared to
explanation: Used to compare two things
translation_1: It improved compared to last year
translation_2: The quality is good compared to the price

title: depending on
explanation: Used to show variation based on condition
translation_1: It changes depending on the weather
translation_2: Opinions differ depending on the person

title: instead of
explanation: Used to express substitution
translation_1: I drink tea instead of coffee

title: not only but also
explanation: Used to express addition of another fact
translation_1: He is not only smart but also kind
example_2: 이 책은 재미있을 뿐만 아니라 유익해요
translation_2: This book is not only interesting but also useful

title: on the other hand
explanation: Used to contrast two situations
translation_1: I like it, on the other hand my friend dislikes it
translation_2: The city is busy while the countryside is quiet

title: as much as
explanation: Used to express degree or proportion
translation_1: The result is good as much as I worked hard
translation_2: You gain weight as much as you eat

title: in addition
explanation: Used to add more information
translation_1: Not only is it raining, but also windy
translation_2: He is handsome and also has a good personality

title: the reason is
explanation: Used to explain a reason
translation_1: The reason I was late is traffic
translation_2: The reason I study is because I have a goal

title: as a result
explanation: Used to show result of an action
translation_1: As a result of studying hard, I passed
translation_2: As a result of effort, I succeeded

title: among
explanation: Used to indicate a situation within a group
translation_1: I found him among many people
translation_2: I made a decision among confusion

title: since/now that
explanation: Used when a condition is already true
translation_1: Since we started, we must finish
translation_2: Now that you promised, you must keep it

title: plan to
explanation: Used to express a planned action
translation_2: I plan to have a meeting

title: possibility
explanation: Used to express possibility
translation_1: There is a possibility of rain
translation_2: There is a possibility a problem may occur

title: have no choice
explanation: Used to express having no other option
translation_1: I had no choice but to take a taxi because I was late
example_2: 비가 와서 집에 있을 수밖에 없어요
translation_2: I had no choice but to stay home because it rained

title: might
explanation: Used to express uncertainty or possibility

title: should have
explanation: Used to express regret about a past action

title: thought would
explanation: Used to express expectation that something would happen

title: didn’t know would
explanation: Used to express surprise or unexpected result
translation_1: I didn’t know it would be this difficult
translation_2: I didn’t know he would come

title: reported speech
explanation: Used to report a statement

title: command speech
explanation: Used to report a command
translation_1: The teacher told me to study

title: suggestion speech
explanation: Used to report a suggestion

title: don’t know if
explanation: Used to express uncertainty about something
translation_1: I don’t know if he is coming
translation_2: I don’t know if it is correct

title: know if
explanation: Used to express knowing about something
translation_1: I know if he is coming
translation_2: I know if there is a problem

title: every time
explanation: Used to express repetition whenever something happens
translation_1: I smile every time I see it
translation_2: I feel happy every time I meet him

title: while in state
explanation: Used to maintain a state while doing something
translation_1: I slept with my eyes closed
translation_2: I went out with the light on

title: while at it
explanation: Used to do something additional while already doing something
translation_2: Since I went, I met a friend

title: thanks to
explanation: Used to express a positive result
translation_1: Thanks to your help, I finished
translation_2: Thanks to the weather, I feel good

title: as a result
explanation: Used to express result of an extreme situation
translation_1: I was so tired that I fell asleep
translation_2: I was so nervous that I couldn’t speak

title: for the purpose of
explanation: Used to express doing something for multiple purposes
translation_2: I went to the library to study

title: just only
explanation: Used to emphasize that it is only that

title: nothing but
explanation: Used to emphasize a simple fact

title: rather than
explanation: Used to choose one option over another
translation_1: Rather than go, I stay home

title: to the extent that
explanation: Used to express degree
translation_1: I was sad enough to cry
translation_2: I worked to the point of exhaustion
